theory.canonical_statement: A unified Lagrangian density combines gravity, quantum fields, topological terms and informational dynamics: L\_U = (c\^{}4/(16 pi G)) R plus L\_QFT{[}psi, A{]} plus L\_topo{[}A{]} plus L\_info{[}Phi{]}.
theory.technical_interpretation: A unified Lagrangian density combines gravity, quantum fields, topological terms and informational dynamics: L\_U = (c\^{}4/(16 pi G)) R plus L\_QFT{[}psi, A{]} plus L\_topo{[}A{]} plus L\_info{[}Phi{]}.

canonical_equation.source_section_latex:
\[
S = |E(\theta_1, \theta_2) - E(\theta_1, \theta_2')| + |E(\theta_1', \theta_2) + E(\theta_1', \theta_2')| > 2
\]
\[
Omega_{FD} = \frac{GJ}{c^2 r^3} \quad \text{(analog with } J = I\omega_{gyro}\text{)}
\]

equation_progression.interpretive_role: This gathers the sectors that the tertiary arc is trying to hold in one variational frame.
This turns the density into an actual field law rather than a formal sum.
This closes the entry by imposing stationary admissibility on the total action.
